articles.title: Leonardo DiCaprio ignored John C. Reilly's advice to skip 'Titanic' and it changed his career forever
articles.description: John C. Reilly famously advised Leonardo DiCaprio against starring in 'Titanic', believing its predictable plot about a sinking ship wouldn't resonate. He urged DiCaprio to choose Paul Thomas Anderson's 'Boogie Nights' instead. DiCaprio, however, defied the advice, leading to 'Titanic's' monumental success and his global stardom, a decision he now acknowledges but never regrets.

articles.title: On Cam | Massive Buildings Reduced To Debris After 7.8 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Philippines
articles.description: The epicenter was 13 km (8 miles) southwest of General Santos city on the island of Mindanao and had a depth of 10 km (6.2 miles).

articles.title: Startup Founder Highlights Rental Bias In Bengaluru, Calls It "Nightmare"
articles.description: Many social media users agreed and pointed out that bachelors are often turned down by landlords who prefer families or married couples.

articles.title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for June 8, 2026 week - check list
articles.description: Stock market recommendations: Shriram Finance, Cummins India are the top stocks that Motilal Oswal Wealth Management Research Desk has recommended for the week starting June 8, 2026. The brokerage has also shared target levels and possible upside.

articles.title: Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner’s Albania luxury resort plan sparks massive protests - What we know so far
articles.description: Protests erupt in Albania against a massive luxury resort planned by Jared Kushner and Ivanka Trump. Thousands demonstrate against the $6.1 billion project on protected coastal land. Environmental groups warn of ecosystem damage and habitat loss. The Albanian government supports the investment, calling it crucial for the nation. The controversy highlights a clash between development and conservation.

articles.title: Cuba prepares for war while US can’t keep up with losses from Iranian proxies? Big details out
articles.description: Are the U.S. and Cuba heading toward their most dangerous confrontation in decades? Reports claim Havana is preparing civilians for potential conflict as Washington ramps up sanctions and pressure on Cuba’s leadership. President Donald Trump recently suggested Cuba has “sort of collapsed,” while new sanctions targeted top Cuban officials and relatives of former leader Raul Castro. Meanwhile, Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel accused the U.S. of waging economic warfare and trying to force national collapse. As tensions escalate across the Caribbean, fears of a new Cuba crisis are growing rapidly.

articles.title: Mother gets emotional as she flies on plane piloted by her son: ‘Watching world feels different today’
articles.description: A woman had shared an emotional moment after flying on a plane piloted by her son.

articles.title: Bengaluru man moves to Mumbai, says his biggest culture shock wasn't the weather or rents
articles.description: A Bengaluru professional's move to Mumbai led to an unexpected realization about auto drivers. After a driver declined his ride request, he unexpectedly offered to help Kumar reach the main road. This simple act of kindness, followed by a smooth, metered ride with a fair fare, left a lasting impression, highlighting the helpfulness of strangers.

articles.title: Knox Jolie-Pitt channels dad Brad Pitt's 'Fight Club' energy in Muay Thai match - WATCH
articles.description: Angelina Jolie's son, Knox, recently showcased impressive Muay Thai skills in a viral video, drawing comparisons to his father, Brad Pitt's 'Fight Club' persona. Attended by his mother and twin sister, the teen fighter entered the ring on his graduation day, even inviting classmates to witness his bout. Knox, largely private, has been dedicated to martial arts training.

articles.title: Bhutan experiences 5.8-magnitude earthquake; tremors felt in parts of Northeast India
articles.description: South Asia News: NEW DELHI: A magnitude 5.8 earthquake struck Bhutan late on Sunday night, triggering multiple aftershocks and sending tremors across parts of Northeas.

articles.title: After Beirut bombed again, Iran warns Israel to 'watch the sky tonight' as fears of retaliation grow
articles.description: Tensions surged after Israel launched fresh strikes on Beirut’s Dahiyeh suburb, prompting a dramatic warning from Iran. Iranian lawmaker Ebrahim Rezaei vowed a “decisive and painful” response and urged Israelis to “watch the sky tonight,” fueling fears of imminent retaliation. The threat came after the I.D.F. said it targeted Hezbollah infrastructure following rocket fire from Lebanon. As residents fled parts of Beirut fearing more bombardments, concerns grew that the fragile ceasefire framework could collapse. With attacks continuing across southern Lebanon, the risk of a wider regional confrontation is once again rising sharply.

articles.title: News in Pics | June 8, 2026: Best Photos from around the world
TDP finalises 3 names for Upper House
articles.description: The Telugu Desam Party has selected three candidates for the upcoming Rajya Sabha elections. Sana Sathish Babu will be renominated. Other chosen candidates are educationist Bhashyam Ramakrishna and Vijay Chinthakayala, son of Andhra Pradesh Speaker Chinthakayala Ayyana Patrudu. The NDA alliance secured a majority in the assembly elections, enabling them to claim four Rajya Sabha seats.

articles.title: NFL Trade Rumor: Cincinnati Bengals and Green Bay Packers emerge as top landing spots for $75M New Orleans Saints star
articles.description: The New Orleans Saints’ star player Alvin Kamara has dominated headlines as he is speculated to be traded ahead of the new season sparking chaos among fans. While a number of teams have been linked to him in the last few weeks, the Cincinnati Bengals and the Green Bay Packers have emerged as potential landing destinations for the star player.

articles.title: Christian Eriksen collapses again during Denmark-Ukraine friendly match
articles.description: Danish footballer Christian Eriksen collapsed on the pitch again during a friendly against Ukraine. He is conscious and stable. The incident recalls his cardiac arrest at Euro 2021. The friendly match has been called off.

articles.title: J&K LG flags off 'Nasha Mukt' padyatra, urges collective action
articles.description: J&K LG Manoj Sinha flagged off the Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan padyatra in Poonch, urging collective action against drug abuse. The campaign aims to raise awareness, strengthen preventive measures, and ensure community participation in the fight against addiction.

articles.title: Iran launches missiles at Israel, first attack since April 8 ceasefire
Pune: 156 detained as police bust rave party in Tulapur; Ganja seized
articles.description: Pune Crime Branch raided an alleged rave party in Tulapur, detaining 156 people. The raid, conducted on June 7, 2026, led to the seizure of ganja, illegal liquor, and banned hookah flavours. Two event organisers have been identified.

articles.title: TMC is collapsing, Mamata Banerjee losing grip on party, claims BJP
articles.description: BJP leaders claim the Trinamool Congress is collapsing and Mamata Banerjee is losing control. They cite internal defiance, arrests of TMC leaders like Saokat Molla, and cracks within the larger INDIA alliance as evidence of the party's decline.

articles.title: SP MP decries 'mosque' demolition in Sambhal; police cite illegal construction
articles.description: SP MP Zia Ur Rehman Barq decried the demolition of a '150-year-old mosque' in Sambhal, UP, as a violation of rights. Police state it was an illegal structure on cemetery land where controversial posters and a flag were found.

articles.title: Sunny Deol shares adorable pic of mom Prakash Kaur kissing Bobby Deol
articles.description: Sunny Deol praised his brother Bobby Deol's performance in 'Bandar' by sharing a heartwarming picture of their mother Prakash Kaur kissing Bobby. Esha Deol also lauded her brother, calling him 'undoubtedly the finest actor today'.

articles.title: Case registered over caste discrimination complaint by woman in Thiruvananthapuram
articles.description: A woman in Thiruvananthapuram files a caste discrimination complaint against a school bus coordinator, leading to police investigation.

articles.title: Rudraprayag: Megha Engineers' stone crusher seized for illegal ops
articles.description: A stone crusher of Megha Engineers, linked to the Rishikesh-Karnaprayag Railway Project, was seized in Rudraprayag for illegal operations. The crusher's validity had expired, and the company also owes Rs 4.5 crore in mining revenue.

articles.title: LPG price hike: TS Singh Deo warns of pressure amid West Asia crisis
articles.description: Congress leader TS Singh Deo linked the domestic LPG price hike to the West Asia crisis, urging the Centre for transparency. The government defended the move, while Karnataka Minister Yathindra Siddaramaiah also criticised the decision.

articles.title: ITBP sub-inspector dies by suicide, jumps from Patna hospital roof
articles.description: An ITBP sub-inspector, Vijay Singh, allegedly died by suicide in Patna by jumping from a hospital roof. He was undergoing treatment at the Asian City Hospital for a health issue, and his condition had reportedly deteriorated recently.

articles.title: Jannat Zubair’s home tour shows why display crockery is back as a décor trend
articles.description: Designers today use Gold Leafing strategically to create focal points that instantly elevate the luxury quotient of a room.

articles.title: Kerala CM seeks PM's help for Indian nurses facing visa crisis in UAE
articles.description: Kerala CM VD Satheesan urges PM Modi's intervention for Indian healthcare workers, mainly from Kerala, facing visa restrictions and job loss in the UAE after the Iranian Hospital in Dubai closed amid regional tensions.

articles.title: ‘Dangerous ideologies’: Hegseth denounces European countries over migration ‘invasion’ in D-Day speech
Pepe Marti shares behind-the-scenes moments from his maiden Monaco podium: “This is the first of many, many trophies”
articles.description: Cupra Kiro driver Pepe Marti has shared a nine-minute YouTube vlog giving fans a behind-the-scenes look at his first Formula E podium. It shows all that went about in the paddock before and after the Round 9 race in Monaco. Interestingly, the video does not feature teammate Dan Ticktum, who lost his podium finish to Marti after receiving a post-race penalty.

articles.title: Another man dies of 'drug overdose' at Mumbai concert
articles.description: A 28-year-old man died after falling ill at an overnight music concert in Mumbai's Worli. Vrishabh Mahendra Gangurde was attending the 'Klangkuenstler All Night Long' event when he and a female companion experienced health issues. Police are investigating the circumstances, including potential alcohol or substance consumption, and awaiting post-mortem results.

articles.title: Fresh controversy erupts over handling of crucial evidence in Twisha Sharma case as court filings raise questions
articles.description: Doctors documented a double reddish patterned ligature mark around the upper third of her neck and concluded that the cause of death was "antemortem hanging by ligature."

articles.description: Karnataka PGCET 2026 hall ticket expected soon on the KEA official website.

articles.title: World’s 7 deadliest desert Snakes you pray never cross paths with!
articles.description: Deserts, often perceived as barren, harbor some of Earth's most dangerous snakes. These resilient reptiles have evolved potent venom and unique survival strategies to thrive in extreme conditions. From Australia's Inland Taipan, boasting the world's most toxic venom, to North Africa's Saharan Horned Viper and America's Sidewinder, discover the lethal adaptations of these desert dwellers.

articles.title: 'Children of Same River': Nepal Eyes Deeper Ties with India, Seeks Diplomatic Solution to Boundary Row
articles.description: Nepal is important for India in the context of its overall strategic interests in the region, and leaders of the two countries have often noted the age-old "Roti Beti" relationship.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by George Harrison: ‘You've got as many lives as you like, and more, even ones...’ Master musician's wisdom on life and evolution
articles.description: George Harrison, the influential guitarist of The Beatles, believed life offers numerous "lives" beyond a single, fixed journey. His quote reflects his interest in spirituality and continuous personal transformation, suggesting that individuals navigate multiple roles, challenges, and circumstances. This perspective encourages acceptance of inevitable change and embraces life as a journey of reinvention and discovery.

articles.title: India’s baby bust: Why birth rates are falling and why Elon Musk is concerned
articles.description: The decline was highlighted in the 2025 State of World Population Report by the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), which estimated India’s fertility rate at 1.9 births per woman.

articles.title: Point, Click, Build: Cursor’s new design mode makes AI coding more intuitive
articles.description: New feature allows developers to point, click and annotate live applications so AI agents can understand design changes more accurately.

articles.title: 'People sitting abroad can't dictate India's youth': Nitin Nabin says young Indians won't be puppets
articles.description: Nabin said some individuals operating from abroad believed they could influence India's youth, but maintained that young Indians would not become "puppets" in anyone's hands.

articles.title: Ashwin on Suryakumar's T20I snub: A significant precedent set
articles.description: R Ashwin voiced concern over Suryakumar Yadav's T20I omission, calling it a 'landmark' precedent. He questioned dropping a T20 World Cup-winning captain and the appointment of Shreyas Iyer from outside the squad.

articles.title: US treasury department may use Iranian assets to pay for damages to Gulf allies: Report
articles.description: Several Gulf countries which host US military bases have come under fire from Iran during the course of the war and have sustained heavy losses.

articles.title: Donald Trump amplifies bizarre AI music video portraying him as an icon
articles.description: Donald Trump shared a surreal AI-generated music video on Truth Social. Created by a Republican candidate, it depicts Trump as a global icon in bizarre scenarios like riding a lion and being on the moon, with lyrics praising his global popularity.

articles.title: LPG price hike due to global crisis, less in India: Bihar BJP chief
articles.description: Bihar BJP chief Sanjay Saraogi defended the LPG price hike, attributing it to global factors. He credited PM Modi's leadership for the rise being less in India. He also slammed the INDIA bloc for infighting ahead of their meeting.

articles.title: WI vs SL 2nd ODI abandoned due to rain; hosts' series hopes dashed
articles.description: Persistent rain at Sabina Park forced the abandonment of the second ODI between West Indies and Sri Lanka. West Indies won the toss but no ball was bowled. The washout ends the hosts' series victory hopes, with Sri Lanka maintaining their 1-0 lead.

articles.title: Tehri Garhwal: Bus overturns near Kaudiyala, 8 sustain minor injuries
articles.description: A bus with 39 passengers overturned near Rishikesh; 8 suffered minor injuries. In a separate Haridwar accident, a pilgrim bus was hit by a dumper, killing one woman and injuring 26 others. Both incidents are under investigation.

articles.title: J-K: Army jawan dies after slipping from rock during Rajouri op
articles.description: An Indian Army jawan lost his life after accidentally slipping from a rock during 'Operation Sheruwali' in Rajouri, J&K. He sustained serious injuries in the fall and later succumbed. The anti-terror op has been underway for over 16 days.

articles.title: The men who walk the tracks at night: Konkan Railway gears up for monsoon
articles.description: Railway Patrolmen: Discover how patrolmen secure the Konkan Railway during monsoon, ensuring safety in rugged terrains.
